evidence.q1: Sections are sequentially numbered 1 through 15 with no gaps, e.g., '## 1. Roles and Scope', '## 2. Definitions', … , '## 15. Term and Transition Assistance'.
evidence.q2: The defined term 'Personal Data' appears capitalized in Section 2 and later sections, but Section 1 uses uncapitalized 'personal data' (e.g., 'For personal data processed under this DPA' and 'This DPA applies to all personal data submitted to…'), inconsistent with the defined term's usage.
evidence.q3: Timeframes use specific business-day limits, e.g., 'within 2 business days' (Section 3) and 'within 5 business days' (Section 8); no vague or non‑commercial phrasing.
evidence.q4: Register is modern commercial prose without archaic legalese, e.g., 'Vendor will not use Personal Data for Vendor's own analytics, benchmarking, product development, model training, advertising, or other independent business purposes.'
evidence.q5: Sentences are grammatically sound and non‑contradictory, e.g., 'This DPA applies to all personal data submitted to, accessed by, or generated through the records normalization service on behalf of Customer.'
evidence.q6: Phrasing and tone remain consistent across sections; compare 'Vendor will process Personal Data only on documented instructions from Customer' (Section 3) with 'Vendor will not transfer Personal Data to another country unless a lawful transfer mechanism is in place' (Section 12).
